channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Electrically writing a magnetic heliknoton in a chiral magnet
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 07 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02450-0

Magnetic heliknotons are hopfions embedded in helical spin backgrounds. Current-induced nucleation and Hall-effect-free motion of isolated magnetic heliknotons is demonstrated in the chiral magnet FeGe.
item.dc:title: Electrically writing a magnetic heliknoton in a chiral magnet
item.dc:creator: Long Li
Dongsheng Song
Weiwei Wang
Lingyao Kong
Shuisen Zhang
Ning Wang
Shilei Zhang
Mingliang Tian
Jiadong Zang
Yizhou Liu
Haifeng Du

item.title: All-nitride superconducting qubits based on atomic layer deposition
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02448-8

NbN/AlN/NbN qubits prepared by atomic layer deposition allow for precise control of the tunnelling barrier and stable operation beyond the temperature limit of aluminium-based devices.
item.dc:title: All-nitride superconducting qubits based on atomic layer deposition
item.dc:creator: Danqing Wang
Yufeng Wu
Naomi Pieczulewski
Prachi Garg
Manuel C. C. Pace
Charlotte G. L. Bøttcher
Baishakhi Mazumder
David A. Muller
Hong X. Tang

item.title: Imaging supermoiré relaxation in helical trilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 05 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02423-3

A scanning single-electron transistor is used to probe the strain dependence of moiré and supermoiré domains. It is observed that these can be considered nearly independent of each other.
item.dc:title: Imaging supermoiré relaxation in helical trilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Jesse C. Hoke
Yifan Li
Yuwen Hu
Julian May-Mann
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Trithep Devakul
Benjamin E. Feldman

item.title: A metastable tetragonal phase in two-dimensional halide perovskite lattices driven by a coherent Higgs mode
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 05 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02433-1

Light-induced phonon dynamics in 2D (BA)2PbI4 films reveal transient lattice shifts to a tetragonal phase, driven by Higgs-mode vibrations. Below-bandgap excitation causes larger spectral shifts, showing strong optomechanical coupling in perovskites.
item.dc:title: A metastable tetragonal phase in two-dimensional halide perovskite lattices driven by a coherent Higgs mode
item.dc:creator: Ayushi Shukla
Sraddha Agrawal
Shoshanna Peifer
Mercouri G. Kanatzidis
Pierre Darancet
Richard D. Schaller
